---
title: "4.8 FRQ Digits Challenge"
subtitle: "ArrayList and its Methods"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit4-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Groupwork Coding Challenge

### FRQ Digits

This coding challenge is based on the 2017 AP CSA exam FRQ part 1a.

In this question, you write a constructor for a class called `Digits`.

The constructor takes an integer number as its argument, divides it into digits, and puts the digits into an `ArrayList`.

For example, `new Digits(154)` creates an `ArrayList` with the digits `[1, 5, 4]`.

## Digits With / and %

### Integer division and remainder

What happens if you divide an integer by `10`?

Integer division truncates everything to the right of the decimal point.

Which digit can you get by using `% 10`, which returns the remainder after dividing by `10`?

## Coding Exercise

### `activecode:: divideby10`

Set `number` to a different number and guess what `/` and `%` will return.

Which operator gives you a digit in `number`?

```java
public class DivideBy10
{
    public static void main(String[] args)
    {
        int number = 154;
        System.out.println(number / 10);
        System.out.println(number % 10);
    }
}
```

## divideby10 Test Target

### Runestone check

The source test checks that the original code has been changed.

Use this page as a prediction-and-run checkpoint:

::: {.tight-list}
- `number / 10` cuts off the last digit
- `number % 10` gives the last digit
:::

## Digit Loop Pseudocode

### Reverse order first

We can use a `while` loop to print out each digit in reverse order starting from the right.

For `154`, that order is `4, 5, 1`.

Pseudocode:

::: {.tight-list}
- while number is greater than `0`
- print out the last digit using `%`
- change the number to cut off the last digit using `/`
:::

## Digits Constructor Idea

### Add digits to an ArrayList

Now write a constructor for the `Digits` class that uses this loop and adds each found digit to the `ArrayList` instead of printing it.

This creates the digit list in reverse order.

To get the digits in the right order, use `add(index, obj)` to add the digit to the beginning of the `ArrayList` instead of the end.

## Coding Challenge

### `activecode:: challenge-digits`

Complete the challenge below to put the digits of a number in an `ArrayList`.

## Digits Starter

::: {.code-scroll}
```java
import java.util.*;

public class Digits
{
    /** A list of digits */
    private ArrayList<Integer> digitList;

    /** Constructs a list of digits from the given number */
    public Digits(int number)
    {
        // initialize digitList to an empty ArrayList of Integers

        // Use a while loop to add each digit in number to digitList
        //   use add(index,object) with the index 0 to add the
        //   digit to the beginning of the list instead of the end


    }

    /** returns the string representation of the digits list */
    public String toString()
    {
        return digitList.toString();
    }

    public static void main(String[] args)
    {
        Digits d1 = new Digits(154);
        System.out.println(d1);
    }
}
```
:::

## Digits Test Targets

### Runestone checks

Expected output from `main`:

```text
[1, 5, 4]
```

Additional test:

```text
new Digits(123456).toString() -> [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6]
```

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should include

::: {.tight-list}
- use `/ 10` to remove the last digit
- use `% 10` to get the last digit
- loop while `number > 0`
- initialize `digitList` as an empty `ArrayList<Integer>`
- use `digitList.add(0, digit)` so the final list is in normal order
- confirm `Digits(154)` produces `[1, 5, 4]`
:::

## End

### 4.8 Part 4 complete

Next: ArrayList traversals.
